200.q: What nickname is Walter White Jr. often called?
What state is Breaking Bad set in?
How many seasons does Breaking Bad have?
What is the name of the car wash the Whites buy?
What fast food restaurant does Gus Fring own?
What year did Breaking Bad premiere?
What subject does Walter White teach?
What color is their signature meth?
Where does Breaking Bad take place?

200.a: Walter White Jr. — goes by Flynn
Gus Fring's fried chicken restaurant chain — a front for his drug empire

200.q: What car does Walter buy impulsively after first cook?
200.a: A Pontiac Aztek — later a Dodge Challenger
Drug Enforcement Administration — the federal agency Hank works for

200.q: What series prequel spinoff is Breaking Bad known for?
Who does Walter ultimately die trying to save?
200.a: Jesse Pinkman — he rescues him from neo-Nazis
One of Jesse's friends who is shot while selling meth
Brandon Mayhew — one of Jesse's goofy friends

200.q: What is Walt's business partner before the show?
200.a: Elliot Schwartz — co-founder of Gray Matter Technologies
A Southern US expression meaning to go wrong or deviate from the straight and narrow
Jesse's girlfriend who is killed by Todd as a message to Jesse
One of Jesse's friends — a surprisingly skilled pianist

200.q: Where does Walt bury his money?
200.a: In the desert, coordinated using lottery ticket GPS coordinates

400.q: What fake company is used to hide the superlab laundry operation?
400.a: Lavanderia Brillante

400.q: What poison plant does Walter use on Brock?
400.a: Lily of the Valley

400.q: What is Mike Ehrmantraut's granddaughter's name?
400.a: Kaylee

400.q: What is Saul Goodman's real surname?
400.a: McGill

400.q: What is Walter White's car at the start of the series?
400.a: Pontiac Aztek

400.q: What company extermination front does Walt use to cook in houses?
400.a: Vamonos Pest

400.q: What is Skyler and Walt's daughter named?
400.a: Holly

400.q: What is Gus Fring's full name?
400.a: Gustavo Fring

400.q: Who plays Gus Fring?
400.a: Giancarlo Esposito

400.q: What kills Gus Fring?
400.a: A pipe bomb (attached to Hector's wheelchair)

400.q: What is the name of Mike's granddaughter?
400.a: Kaylee

400.q: What is Tuco Salamanca's uncle's name?
400.a: Hector Salamanca

400.q: What song does Walter sing when he's in the crawl space?
400.a: He laughs maniacally

400.q: What RV brand do Walt and Jesse cook in?
400.a: Fleetwood Bounder

400.q: What is the name of Jesse's girlfriend who dies of overdose?
400.a: Jane Margolis

400.q: What does Hank collect as a hobby?
400.a: Minerals (rocks)

400.q: What is the name of the law firm where Saul works?
400.a: Saul Goodman & Associates

400.q: What pseudonym does Walter Jr. use?
400.a: Flynn

400.q: Who kills Hank Schrader?
400.a: Jack Welker

400.q: Who is Walter's brother-in-law?
400.a: Hank Schrader — a DEA agent who unknowingly hunts his own family

400.q: What is Walter's former business worth billions?
400.a: Gray Matter Technologies — he sold his share for $5,000

400.q: How does Gus Fring die?
400.a: A pipe bomb rigged under Hector Salamanca's wheelchair

400.q: What acid does Walter use to dissolve bodies?
400.a: Hydrofluoric acid (HF)

400.q: Who kills Mike Ehrmantraut?
400.a: Walter White — in a fit of rage by the river

400.q: What is the fate of Walter White?
400.a: He dies from a gunshot wound after rescuing Jesse and exposing the neo-Nazis

400.q: What is the blue meth's purity?
400.a: 99.1% pure — almost impossibly high quality

400.q: Who is Gustavo Fring?
400.a: A powerful drug kingpin hiding behind his Pollos Hermanos restaurant chain

400.q: What is the significance of the fly episode?
400.a: Walter becomes obsessed with a fly — symbolising guilt, contamination, and loss of control

400.q: What did Walter do to Jane?
400.a: He watched her choke to death without intervening — one of his most damning acts

400.q: What is Walter's final confession to Skyler?
400.a: I did it for me. I liked it. I was good at it. And I was really... I was alive

400.q: Who is Lydia Rodarte-Quayle?
400.a: A Madrigal Electromotive executive connecting Gus's operation to an international supply chain

400.q: How does Hank die?
400.a: He is shot by Jack Welker in the desert after being captured

400.q: Who is the vacuum cleaner man?
400.a: Ed Galbraith — a man who creates new identities and helps people disappear

400.q: What is the Vamonos Pest arc?
400.a: Walter and Mike use a pest control company as a front — tenting houses as mobile meth labs

400.q: Who is Tio Salamanca?
400.a: Hector Salamanca — a wheelchair-bound former cartel boss who communicates by ringing a bell

400.q: What is the poisoning of Brock about?
400.a: Walter uses Lily of the Valley to poison Brock — to manipulate Jesse into helping kill Gus

400.q: Who inherits Walter's money at the end?
400.a: Walter Jr. receives it through the Schwartz trust — blood money Walter tries to make right

400.q: What happens to Jesse at the end?
400.a: He escapes, driving away screaming — his story continues in El Camino

400.q: Who is the neo-Nazi uncle?
400.a: Jack Welker

400.q: What is the significance of the White Supremacist gang?
400.a: Jack Welker and Todd take over the cook — imprisoning Jesse as their slave cook

400.q: What is the methylamine used for?
400.a: The key precursor chemical for Walter's unique blue meth synthesis

400.q: Who is Saul Goodman really?
400.a: Jimmy McGill — later runs a Cinnabon in Omaha as Gene Takavic (Better Call Saul)

400.q: What is the Vamonos Pest front?
400.a: A pest control company used as cover — they tent houses and cook meth inside

400.q: Why does Walter's cancer return in Season 5?
400.a: His cancer progresses again — giving him little time to live and nothing left to lose

600.a: Fe-Li-Na = Iron, Lithium, Sodium (blood, meth, tears)

600.q: Who directed the pilot of Breaking Bad?
What is the maximum purity Walter's meth reaches?
What spin-off series follows Saul Goodman?
What is the name of the cartel don who Gus kills?
What poison does Walter use on Brock?
What fake name does Walter use to buy a gun?
What confession does Walter make in the finale?
600.a: He did it for himself, because he liked it

600.q: Who is the only Salamanca cousin to survive?
What year does the series finale take place?
What chemistry makes their meth blue?
600.a: Their P2P reductive amination synthesis produces a racemic mixture — the unique route gives it a blue tint

600.q: What is the Heisenberg name reference?
600.a: Werner Heisenberg — quantum physicist famous for the Uncertainty Principle, reflecting Walt's unpredictability

600.q: What is El Camino about?
600.a: Jesse gets money from a safe, buys a new identity from Ed the disappearer, and moves to Alaska

600.q: What does Walter's hat symbolise?
600.a: The porkpie hat symbolises his transformation from mild teacher to criminal kingpin — the Heisenberg identity

600.q: What is the pink teddy bear's significance in Season 2?
600.a: It connects to a Wayfarer airline crash — caused by Jane's grief-stricken father — all consequences of Walter's choices

600.q: How does Walter poison Brock?
600.a: Using Lily of the Valley berries — after Huell pickpockets Jesse's ricin cigarette; Walter blames Gus

600.q: What is the full arc of Jesse Pinkman?
600.a: From small-time meth cook to enslaved cook to free man — a journey of trauma, guilt, and liberation

600.q: What is the Ozymandias episode about?
600.a: The fallout of the desert shootout — Hank's death, Walt's empire collapsing, the family's destruction

600.q: What philosophical concept does Breaking Bad illustrate?
600.a: The banality of evil — an ordinary man makes rationalised choices that lead to becoming genuinely evil

600.q: What is the neo-Nazi cook vs Walt's cook?
600.a: Todd can cook but can't replicate the blue — his meth is inferior, forcing them to keep Jesse alive

600.q: Who is Huell and what is his most famous moment?
600.a: Saul's bodyguard — famous for lying sprawled on Walter's money, grinning contentedly

600.q: What is Gus Fring's backstory?
600.a: His partner Max was killed by the Salamancas — his entire drug empire was built around revenge against Don Eladio
